title: Propagate GitHub token into canonical conversation secrets
description:
Problem
After removing `/run`, the GitHub Worker no longer forwards the resolved GitHub token to the agent-server conversation path. That means tools running inside the canonical conversation path cannot use `GITHUB_TOKEN`, even though the Worker already resolved the correct installation or user token.

Why this matters
- GitHub-aware tools need the token inside the agent runtime, not just in the Worker.
- This was previously available through the bespoke `/run` envelope and got dropped during the refactor.
- Gemini correctly flagged this as a real regression.

Desired outcome
The canonical conversation path should receive the GitHub token as a secret, using the same `GITHUB_TOKEN` name that the agent-server already knows how to register.

title: Add local machine and GitHub invocation context to agent-server environment information
description:
Problem:
The canonical agent-server conversation path does not currently inject local machine facts or GitHub invocation metadata into the system prompt.

Desired outcome:
Use the existing system-prompt environment-information seam to tell the agent:
- local repo layout (repos live under ~/repos, smolpaws lives at ~/repos/smolpaws)
- the resolved workspace root for this conversation
- GitHub invocation context such as repo, event type, actor, and issue/PR number when present

Constraint:
Keep this simple. Do not add a new prompt system or a full skills-loading subsystem just for this.

title: Support notifications-path mentions outside app-installed repos
description:
Problem
A mention in a repo where the GitHub App is not installed should still be able to trigger SmolPaws through the notifications polling path, but the current implementation has multiple blockers.

Observed case
- `@smolpaws` mention in `enyst/.openhands` issue #4 did not trigger a reply even though the `smolpaws` GitHub account can see the notification.

Current blockers
- `GITHUB_USER_TOKEN` is not configured in the Worker secrets, so scheduled notification polling currently returns early.
- The notifications cron is now hourly, so even with a token the path is delayed by design.
- The current notification handler expects `subject.latest_comment_url` and ignores threads without it, which likely skips issue-body mentions in non-app-installed repos.

Desired outcome
The notifications path should reliably process mentions from allowed authors in allowed repos even when the GitHub App is not installed there, including issue-body mentions where that is a real notification shape.

title: Finish neutral scope naming and remove remaining group/main aliases
description:
Problem
The runtime model has been moved to neutral execution scopes, but the WhatsApp host/runtime seam still carries legacy aliases like `groupFolder` and `isMain` in `src/agent-runtime/types.ts`, `src/index.ts`, and `src/task-scheduler.ts`. The mount-security path also still uses `isMain` terminology.

Why this matters
- The codebase conceptually moved from tenancy-by-group toward trusted scopes, but the runtime payloads still tell two stories.
- This makes the remaining security and task code harder to reason about than it needs to be.

Desired outcome
Use `scopeId` / `isControlScope` consistently at the host/runtime boundary and keep any truly unavoidable legacy naming isolated behind one compatibility seam, or remove it entirely if nothing external still needs it.

title: Refresh incoming secrets when reusing a live conversation
description:
Problem
`createConversationRecord(...)` returns early for an existing live conversation and only refreshes the SmolPaws config. New incoming secrets on `POST /api/conversations` are ignored in that path.

Why this matters
- GitHub installation tokens are short-lived and must be refreshed across comments.
- Even if the Worker starts sending `GITHUB_TOKEN`, reused conversations would still keep the stale or missing secret.
- This is a general canonical conversation bug, not just a GitHub-only detail.

Desired outcome
When `POST /api/conversations` reuses a live conversation, the server should also apply the incoming secrets to that conversation before the next user message runs.

title: Add an ingress contract test for the GitHub Worker -> agent-server conversation path
description:
Problem:
We have a live end-to-end GitHub path now, but there is no automated test proving the Cloudflare Worker queue consumer speaks the expected agent-server endpoints and request shapes.

Desired outcome:
Add a lightweight automated test that exercises the GitHub ingress queue-consumer path against the agent-server contract, especially:
- POST /api/conversations
- POST /api/conversations/:id/outbound_messages/claim
- fallback behavior when outbound messages are empty or prompt is blank

Why this matters:
This is the narrowest useful E2E seam before the full networked GitHub path, and it will catch contract drift between apps/github and apps/agent-server.

title: (idea) SmolPaws extension system inspired by Pi
description:
This bead recreates the old GitHub-side idea from `enyst/smolpaws#1`, but it is explicitly for the canonical `smolpaws/smolpaws` repo now.

Goal:
Design a lightweight extension system for SmolPaws, centered on the shared agent-server/runner surface rather than the old standalone GitHub repo.

Original motivation from the old issue:
- Pi-style simple extension entrypoint
- lifecycle hooks around interesting seams (run start, tool call, tool result, run end)
- shareable packages/manifests for tools, skills, prompts, and templates
- support for both local scripts and reusable packaged extensions

Current SmolPaws framing:
- canonical repo is `smolpaws/smolpaws`
- shared runtime surface now lives in the SmolPaws repo (WhatsApp + GitHub ingress converge on the same local agent-server-compatible runtime)
- any extension system should attach at the runner/agent-server boundary and work for both ingress channels

Questions to answer later:
- runner-only extensions vs ingress-specific hooks too
- per-repo vs global vs per-scope extension loading
- capability/safety model for tool interception and credential-sensitive hooks
- whether `send_message` and task tools should be part of the extension capability surface or remain core

This is a memory/parking-lot item for future design work, not an immediate implementation task.

title: Turn Daytona into a workspace backend instead of a special /run path
description:
Problem
Daytona still lives as a special case inside `apps/agent-server/src/agent-server/conversationRouter.ts` via `maybeRunInDaytona(...)`, instead of being modeled as a workspace backend parallel to local Apple-container execution.

Why this matters
- It keeps host lifecycle logic inside the request route instead of the workspace layer.
- It diverges from the Python reference model, where workspaces own runtime lifecycle and the agent-server stays generic.
- It makes the `/run` path carry deployment concerns that should be below the conversation boundary.

Desired outcome
Introduce a Daytona-backed workspace path so both local and Daytona execution target the same agent-server surface, with workspace selection contained in one place and `/run` no longer owning Daytona-specific branching.
notes: Intentionally deferred while we stabilize and test the local plain-runner path. Keep the target architecture aligned with the Python workspace model: workspace owns lifecycle, agent-server stays generic, Daytona becomes a sibling backend to AppleWorkspace rather than a special /run branch.

title: Extract shared runner protocol/types out of agent-server source imports
description:
Problem
`apps/github` imports `../../agent-server/src/shared/github.js` and `../../agent-server/src/shared/runner.js` directly from the agent-server source tree. That works inside the repo, but it means the GitHub app depends on the internal file layout of another app rather than a neutral shared boundary.

Why this matters
- It makes app boundaries fuzzier than they look.
- It raises the cost of refactoring the agent-server package layout.
- It makes future packaging/independent builds more brittle.

Desired outcome
Move the shared queue/runner protocol types and schemas to an explicitly shared module/package owned by `smolpaws`, then make both `apps/github` and `apps/agent-server` consume that boundary instead of cross-importing app internals.

title: Load AgentContext and skills in the agent-server conversation path
description:
Problem
The GitHub and agent-server paths now use the canonical /api/conversations lifecycle, but createConversationRecord(...) still does not attach an AgentContext. That means repo, user, and public skills are still absent from the first LLM request even on the canonical path.

Why this matters
- The route shape is now correct, but the runtime still lacks one of the key Python-style context seams.
- The first LLM turn still misses repo/user/public skill context.
- This is now the main gap behind tool/profile alignment for local repo work.

Desired outcome
The agent-server conversation path should construct and attach an AgentContext that loads the appropriate local skills for the resolved workspace, following the Python model as closely as the TS SDK allows.
notes:
Python reference direction:
- the agent-server exposes a first-class `/skills` endpoint (`skills_router.py`) that loads and merges public, user, org, sandbox, and project skills before the conversation starts
- `StartConversationRequest` then carries an `agent` object that can already include `agent_context`
- TS follow-up should aim at the same shape: a first-class skills-loading seam plus conversation creation that carries the resulting AgentContext, instead of inventing another custom request shortcut

title: Restore real agent capabilities on the standalone /run path
description:
Problem
The current local standalone `/run` path in `apps/agent-server/src/agent-server/conversationRuntime.ts` creates `LocalConversation` with `includeDefaultTools: false`, no `AgentContext`, and only the scope-bound `send_message` tool. That means GitHub-triggered runs can talk back to the thread, but they cannot actually inspect files, run commands, edit code, or load skills on the plain local path.

Why this matters
- The live GitHub mention path currently dispatches through `/run`, so this is the behavior users will see first.
- Even after we bypass Daytona, the agent still will not be able to do real repo work until this path gets a proper tool/context profile.
- It also makes the first LLM request much thinner than the rest of the SDK surface, which is easy to miss when testing only the transport.

Desired outcome
The standalone `/run` path should provision the same meaningful local capabilities we expect from SmolPaws for repo work: default tools (or an explicit workspace-aware profile), plus any required AgentContext/skills for the scope.

title: Add a local shell script for starting the agent-server with required env validation
description:
Problem:
We keep restarting the local agent-server ad hoc, and it is easy to forget required env like LLM_MODEL, provider keys, or runner token.

Desired outcome:
Add a small checked-in shell script to start the local agent-server with clear env validation and stable defaults for local development/testing.

Why this matters:
It should be obvious how to bring up the local runner before testing GitHub/Worker integration.

title: GitHub local runs should resolve a repo workspace, not use one ambient runner root
description:
Problem
`apps/github` sends prompt-based `/run` requests into `apps/agent-server`, but the non-Daytona `/run` path still executes `runStandaloneQuestion(...)` against `deps.env.SMOLPAWS_WORKSPACE_ROOT` instead of a repo-specific checkout/root. In practice that means local GitHub runs can only operate correctly when the runner happens to already be pointed at the right repo.

Why this matters
- GitHub ingress is supposed to target arbitrary allowed repos.
- Without Daytona, the current local path is ambient-root only.
- This is the main remaining functional gap before GitHub local execution is trustworthy.

Current evidence
- `apps/github/src/index.ts` dispatches a generic `/run` request with GitHub context but no local workspace resolution.
- `apps/agent-server/src/agent-server/conversationRouter.ts` falls back from Daytona to `runStandaloneQuestion(...)` with `deps.env.SMOLPAWS_WORKSPACE_ROOT`.
- `docs/github/README.md` already lists non-Daytona repo checkout as remaining work.

Desired outcome
Non-Daytona GitHub runs should resolve or provision a repo-specific workspace on the local machine, following the workspace model rather than relying on one ambient runner root.
